subject: fix(sancio): unblock single-segment routes; harden multi-segment routing
body:
Root cause: api/sancio/[...path].ts read `req.query.path as string[]`. The
cast lied — Vercel's runtime delivers the catch-all param as a bare string
for single-segment URLs, so segments became "projects" instead of
["projects"], no segments[0] branch matched, and every single-segment
sancio request fell through to the explicit 404. The per-handler tests
import resource handlers directly and bypass dispatch, so this never
failed a test.

Changes:
- Add normalizeSegments(raw) at the top of the dispatcher: handles
  string[] (documented shape), bare string (split on / and filter
  Boolean — covers single AND multi-segment string forms), and undefined.
- Add a `console.error("[sancio] fallthrough", { url, method, rawSlug,
  segments })` line before the final 404 so any future regression
  self-diagnoses in one Vercel-logs query.
- vercel.json: drop "framework": "vite". Vite is auto-detected from
  vite.config.ts; declaring it explicitly may invoke SPA-aware behavior
  that interferes with file-based API routing on the platform.
- Rename api/sancio/[...path].ts -> [...slug].ts (via git mv) and read
  req.query.slug. `path` is generic and overlaps with Node/URL
  conventions; `slug` rules out a Vercel-reserved query-key collision
  and reads more clearly. Doc references in CLAUDE.md and API.md updated.

Test coverage:
- New api/__tests__/sancio/dispatcher.test.ts (45 tests). Mocks every
  lib/routes/sancio/* module with named "probe" functions that record
  their name + post-mutation req.query, then drives every documented
  route shape through the dispatcher. Coverage:
    * Input shape normalization: string[], bare string, slash-separated
      string, leading/trailing slashes, missing slug, empty array.
    * Route table: all 26 dispatch branches, asserting both the right
      probe ran AND req.query.id was set for by-id routes.
    * 404 fallthrough: 11 unknown-path cases plus a console.error
      diagnostic assertion.
    * CORS short-circuit: dispatcher returns early without invoking any
      handler when cors() returns true.

Operational follow-up:
- docs/USER_ACTIONS.md: post-deploy verification curl set + Vercel
  support escalation procedure (capture diverging x-vercel-id values
  from a working single-segment call and a failing multi-segment call)
  in case the platform 404 on multi-segment routes persists despite
  the framework + rename changes.

Tags: sancio, routing, vercel, bugfix, testing, dispatcher
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 4
Impact: api
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: sun-sea-o-integration
Semver: patch
